speaker: HAMLET
text: Whither wilt thou lead me? Speak. I’ll go no further.

speaker: GHOST
text: Mark me.

speaker: HAMLET
text: I will.

speaker: GHOST
text: My hour is almost come

speaker: GHOST
text: When I to sulf’rous and tormenting flames

speaker: GHOST
text: Must render up myself.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Alas, poor ghost!

speaker: GHOST
text: Pity me not, but lend thy serious hearing

speaker: GHOST
text: To what I shall unfold.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Speak. I am bound to hear.

speaker: GHOST
text: So art thou to revenge, when thou shalt hear.

speaker: HAMLET
text: What?

speaker: GHOST
text: I am thy father’s spirit,

speaker: GHOST
text: Doomed for a certain term to walk the night

speaker: GHOST
text: And for the day confined to fast in fires

speaker: GHOST
text: Till the foul crimes done in my days of nature

speaker: GHOST
text: Are burnt and purged away. But that I am forbid

speaker: GHOST
text: To tell the secrets of my prison house,

speaker: GHOST
text: I could a tale unfold whose lightest word

speaker: GHOST
text: Would harrow up thy soul, freeze thy young blood,

speaker: GHOST
text: Make thy two eyes, like stars, start from their spheres,

speaker: GHOST
text: Thy knotted and combinèd locks to part,

speaker: GHOST
text: And each particular hair to stand an end,

speaker: GHOST
text: Like quills upon the fearful porpentine.

speaker: GHOST
text: But this eternal blazon must not be

speaker: GHOST
text: To ears of flesh and blood. List, list, O list!

speaker: GHOST
text: If thou didst ever thy dear father love—

speaker: HAMLET
text: O God!

speaker: GHOST
text: Revenge his foul and most unnatural murder.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Murder?

speaker: GHOST
text: Murder most foul, as in the best it is,

speaker: GHOST
text: But this most foul, strange, and unnatural.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Haste me to know ’t, that I, with wings as swift

speaker: HAMLET
text: As meditation or the thoughts of love,

speaker: HAMLET
text: May sweep to my revenge.

speaker: GHOST
text: I find thee apt;

speaker: GHOST
text: And duller shouldst thou be than the fat weed

speaker: GHOST
text: That roots itself in ease on Lethe wharf,

speaker: GHOST
text: Wouldst thou not stir in this. Now, Hamlet, hear.

speaker: GHOST
text: ’Tis given out that, sleeping in my orchard,

speaker: GHOST
text: A serpent stung me. So the whole ear of Denmark

speaker: GHOST
text: Is by a forgèd process of my death

speaker: GHOST
text: Rankly abused. But know, thou noble youth,

speaker: GHOST
text: The serpent that did sting thy father’s life

speaker: GHOST
text: Now wears his crown.

speaker: HAMLET
text: O, my prophetic soul! My uncle!

speaker: GHOST
text: Ay, that incestuous, that adulterate beast,

speaker: GHOST
text: With witchcraft of his wits, with traitorous gifts—

speaker: GHOST
text: O wicked wit and gifts, that have the power

speaker: GHOST
text: So to seduce!—won to his shameful lust

speaker: GHOST
text: The will of my most seeming-virtuous queen.

speaker: GHOST
text: O Hamlet, what a falling off was there!

speaker: GHOST
text: From me, whose love was of that dignity

speaker: GHOST
text: That it went hand in hand even with the vow

speaker: GHOST
text: I made to her in marriage, and to decline

speaker: GHOST
text: Upon a wretch whose natural gifts were poor

speaker: GHOST
text: To those of mine.

speaker: GHOST
text: But virtue, as it never will be moved,

speaker: GHOST
text: Though lewdness court it in a shape of heaven,

speaker: GHOST
text: So, lust, though to a radiant angel linked,

speaker: GHOST
text: Will sate itself in a celestial bed

speaker: GHOST
text: And prey on garbage.

speaker: GHOST
text: But soft, methinks I scent the morning air.

speaker: GHOST
text: Brief let me be. Sleeping within my orchard,

speaker: GHOST
text: My custom always of the afternoon,

speaker: GHOST
text: Upon my secure hour thy uncle stole

speaker: GHOST
text: With juice of cursèd hebona in a vial

speaker: GHOST
text: And in the porches of my ears did pour

speaker: GHOST
text: The leprous distilment, whose effect

speaker: GHOST
text: Holds such an enmity with blood of man

speaker: GHOST
text: That swift as quicksilver it courses through

speaker: GHOST
text: The natural gates and alleys of the body,

speaker: GHOST
text: And with a sudden vigor it doth posset

speaker: GHOST
text: And curd, like eager droppings into milk,

speaker: GHOST
text: The thin and wholesome blood. So did it mine,

speaker: GHOST
text: And a most instant tetter barked about,

speaker: GHOST
text: Most lazar-like, with vile and loathsome crust

speaker: GHOST
text: All my smooth body.

speaker: GHOST
text: Thus was I, sleeping, by a brother’s hand

speaker: GHOST
text: Of life, of crown, of queen at once dispatched,

speaker: GHOST
text: Cut off, even in the blossoms of my sin,

speaker: GHOST
text: Unhouseled, disappointed, unaneled,

speaker: GHOST
text: No reck’ning made, but sent to my account

speaker: GHOST
text: With all my imperfections on my head.

speaker: GHOST
text: O horrible, O horrible, most horrible!

speaker: GHOST
text: If thou hast nature in thee, bear it not.

speaker: GHOST
text: Let not the royal bed of Denmark be

speaker: GHOST
text: A couch for luxury and damnèd incest.

speaker: GHOST
text: But, howsomever thou pursues this act,

speaker: GHOST
text: Taint not thy mind, nor let thy soul contrive

speaker: GHOST
text: Against thy mother aught. Leave her to heaven

speaker: GHOST
text: And to those thorns that in her bosom lodge

speaker: GHOST
text: To prick and sting her. Fare thee well at once.

speaker: GHOST
text: The glowworm shows the matin to be near

speaker: GHOST
text: And ’gins to pale his uneffectual fire.

speaker: GHOST
text: Adieu, adieu, adieu. Remember me.

speaker: HAMLET
text: O all you host of heaven! O Earth! What else?

speaker: HAMLET
text: And shall I couple hell? O fie! Hold, hold, my heart,

speaker: HAMLET
text: And you, my sinews, grow not instant old,

speaker: HAMLET
text: But bear me stiffly up. Remember thee?

speaker: HAMLET
text: Ay, thou poor ghost, whiles memory holds a seat

speaker: HAMLET
text: In this distracted globe. Remember thee?

speaker: HAMLET
text: Yea, from the table of my memory

speaker: HAMLET
text: I’ll wipe away all trivial, fond records,

speaker: HAMLET
text: All saws of books, all forms, all pressures past,

speaker: HAMLET
text: That youth and observation copied there,

speaker: HAMLET
text: And thy commandment all alone shall live

speaker: HAMLET
text: Within the book and volume of my brain,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Unmixed with baser matter. Yes, by heaven!

speaker: HAMLET
text: O most pernicious woman!

speaker: HAMLET
text: O villain, villain, smiling, damnèd villain!

speaker: HAMLET
text: My tables—meet it is I set it down

speaker: HAMLET
text: That one may smile and smile and be a villain.

speaker: HAMLET
text: At least I am sure it may be so in Denmark.

speaker: HAMLET
text: So, uncle, there you are. Now to my word.

speaker: HAMLET
text: It is “adieu, adieu, remember me.”

speaker: HAMLET
text: I have sworn ’t.

speaker: HORATIO
text: My lord, my lord!

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: Lord Hamlet.

speaker: HORATIO
text: Heavens secure him!

speaker: HAMLET
text: So be it.

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: Illo, ho, ho, my lord!

speaker: HAMLET
text: Hillo, ho, ho, boy! Come, bird, come!

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: How is ’t, my noble lord?

speaker: HORATIO
text: What news, my lord?

speaker: HAMLET
text: O, wonderful!

speaker: HORATIO
text: Good my lord, tell it.

speaker: HAMLET
text: No, you will reveal it.

speaker: HORATIO
text: Not I, my lord, by heaven.

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: Nor I, my lord.

speaker: HAMLET
text: How say you, then? Would heart of man once think it?

speaker: HAMLET
text: But you’ll be secret?

speaker:
HORATIO
              /
              MARCELLUS
text: Ay, by heaven, my lord.

speaker: HAMLET
text: There’s never a villain dwelling in all Denmark

speaker: HAMLET
text: But he’s an arrant knave.

speaker: HORATIO
text: There needs no ghost, my lord, come from the grave

speaker: HORATIO
text: To tell us this.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Why, right, you are in the right.

speaker: HAMLET
text: And so, without more circumstance at all,

speaker: HAMLET
text: I hold it fit that we shake hands and part,

speaker: HAMLET
text: You, as your business and desire shall point you

speaker: HAMLET
text: (For every man hath business and desire,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Such as it is), and for my own poor part,

speaker: HAMLET
text: I will go pray.

speaker: HORATIO
text: These are but wild and whirling words, my lord.

speaker: HAMLET
text: I am sorry they offend you, heartily;

speaker: HAMLET
text: Yes, faith, heartily.

speaker: HORATIO
text: There’s no offense, my lord.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Yes, by Saint Patrick, but there is, Horatio,

speaker: HAMLET
text: And much offense, too. Touching this vision here,

speaker: HAMLET
text: It is an honest ghost—that let me tell you.

speaker: HAMLET
text: For your desire to know what is between us,

speaker: HAMLET
text: O’ermaster ’t as you may. And now, good friends,

speaker: HAMLET
text: As you are friends, scholars, and soldiers,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Give me one poor request.

speaker: HORATIO
text: What is ’t, my lord? We will.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Never make known what you have seen tonight.

speaker:
HORATIO
              /
              MARCELLUS
text: My lord, we will not.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Nay, but swear ’t.

speaker: HORATIO
text: In faith, my lord, not I.

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: Nor I, my lord, in faith.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Upon my sword.

speaker: MARCELLUS
text: We have sworn, my lord, already.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Indeed, upon my sword, indeed.

speaker: GHOST
text: Swear.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Ha, ha, boy, sayst thou so? Art thou there, truepenny?

speaker: HAMLET
text: Come on, you hear this fellow in the cellarage.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Consent to swear.

speaker: HORATIO
text: Propose the oath, my lord.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Never to speak of this that you have seen,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Swear by my sword.

speaker: GHOST
text: Swear.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Hic et ubique? Then we’ll shift our ground.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Come hither, gentlemen,

speaker: HAMLET
text: And lay your hands again upon my sword.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Swear by my sword

speaker: HAMLET
text: Never to speak of this that you have heard.

speaker: GHOST
text: Swear by his sword.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Well said, old mole. Canst work i’ th’ earth so fast?—

speaker: HAMLET
text: A worthy pioner! Once more remove, good friends.

speaker: HORATIO
text: O day and night, but this is wondrous strange.

speaker: HAMLET
text: And therefore as a stranger give it welcome.

speaker: HAMLET
text: There are more things in heaven and earth, Horatio,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Than are dreamt of in your philosophy. But come.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Here, as before, never, so help you mercy,

speaker: HAMLET
text: How strange or odd some’er I bear myself

speaker: HAMLET
text: (As I perchance hereafter shall think meet

speaker: HAMLET
text: To put an antic disposition on)

speaker: HAMLET
text: That you, at such times seeing me, never shall,

speaker: HAMLET
text: With arms encumbered thus, or this headshake,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Or by pronouncing of some doubtful phrase,

speaker: HAMLET
text: As “Well, well, we know,” or “We could an if we would,”

speaker: HAMLET
text: Or “If we list to speak,” or “There be an if they might,”

speaker: HAMLET
text: Or such ambiguous giving-out, to note

speaker: HAMLET
text: That you know aught of me—this do swear,

speaker: HAMLET
text: So grace and mercy at your most need help you.

speaker: GHOST
text: Swear.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Rest, rest, perturbèd spirit.—So, gentlemen,

speaker: HAMLET
text: With all my love I do commend me to you,

speaker: HAMLET
text: And what so poor a man as Hamlet is

speaker: HAMLET
text: May do t’ express his love and friending to you,

speaker: HAMLET
text: God willing, shall not lack. Let us go in together,

speaker: HAMLET
text: And still your fingers on your lips, I pray.

speaker: HAMLET
text: The time is out of joint. O cursèd spite

speaker: HAMLET
text: That ever I was born to set it right!

speaker: HAMLET
text: Nay, come, let’s go together.
